rationale: The PowerShell command in A0 uses string concatenation obfuscation and pipes to IEX (Invoke-Expression), which are classic obfuscation/execution techniques; however, DER-001 confirms the deobfuscated payload is merely 'Write-Host Hello, World!' — a benign output command with no confirmed malicious effect. The obfuscation pattern itself warrants suspicion even if the resolved payload is trivial, as it could be a test or proof-of-concept for a delivery mechanism. More concerning are EV-003 and EV-004, where lsass.exe (PID 852) accesses the PowerShell process (PID 3196) with access rights 0x1000 and 0x1478 respectively via an SSPI/RPC call trace — 0x1478 includes PROCESS_VM_READ and other intrusive rights, though the call trace originates from lsasrv.dll/SspiSrv.dll suggesting this may be a legitimate SSPI authentication query rather than credential dumping; no evidence of lsass memory reads or credential extraction is confirmed. The chain (Explorer → cmd → obfuscated IEX PowerShell) under corp\Administrator at High integrity warrants investigation to rule out a loader or red-team activity, but the benign resolved payload and SSPI-consistent call trace cap confidence in a malicious verdict.
